title: hieracium scorzonerifolium subsp. divaricatum
intro: Hieracium scorzonerifolium subsp. divaricatum, commonly known as hawkweed, is a type of flowering plant that belongs to the dandelion family, Asteraceae. This plant is traditionally used in herbal medicine for its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antimicrobial properties, which are attributed to its rich content of sesquiterpene lactones and other bioactive compounds. The medicinal properties of Hieracium scorzonerifolium subsp. divaricatum are said to provide various health benefits, including wound healing, antiseptic effects, and protection against infections. In herbal preparations, the leaves and flowers of this plant are often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to treat various health conditions, including digestive issues and skin problems.
